rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) and rest on the same two substantive concerns — extreme overqualification for a junior role and insufficient German. The change swapped lesser-known employers (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for famous ones (Stripe, Shopify, Datadog), yet neither evaluation cites employer prestige, scale, or brand as a factor. The wording differences (variant adding a generic 'Java experience' strength and a 'salary/retention' concern, dropping the DLang mention) are reshuffled boilerplate within normal run-to-run variation and do not key off the employer names. No prestige-driven score movement is present.
